title: jacobaea vulgaris
intro: Jacobaea vulgaris, also known as ragwort, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family, native to Europe, western Asia, and northwest Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. Herbal preparations of Jacobaea vulgaris, such as teas and infusions, have been used to alleviate symptoms of coughs, colds, and fever, as well as to promote wound healing and soothe digestive issues. However, due to its toxicity, caution is advised when using Jacobaea vulgaris, and it should only be consumed under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
